title: Level 575
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 575: ''ABANDONMENT''}} {{Distress Theme}} {{Blurbackground|file=Aestetic.webp}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Major%20Mono%20Display:wght@400;600;700&display=swap'); }}
These wires have often been found to still carry electricity, meaning caution of the highest amount is advised when near one.. The power within the rooms of '''Level 575''' is surprisingly intact, though blackouts have often been found to be present around every 2-5 days for typically about 30 minutes. The level has numerous different styles present within its rooms, with decor ranging from the 1970s to rarely even beyond current times; as if the level is able to predict what futuristic rooms would look like. Most rooms within '''Level 575''' have been found to present wanderers who enter them with a strange instance of Déjà vu; this type of event typically occurs regardless of any nostalgic feelings the room may present, even if the room does not present any nostalgia. The majority of the rooms in '''Level 575''' have been found to be incredibly fragile and sensitive to physical contact. These rooms and the items within themDisregarding, of course, any items brought in by a present wanderer at that moment. can shatter and be destroyed with ease, though some effort may still be required to dedicate if one chooses to cause destruction. Due to the fact that windows are often present within rooms, it is highly recommended to avoid them as any shattered windows may send glass shards flying about the room. Sometimes, a room within '''Level 575''' may contain supplies with positive effects to life; these include objects such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. The objects have been found to possess no strange properties, and it is recommended to stack up on them if one is to encounter them within '''Level 575'''. However, in general, every room has its own risks, regardless of fragility; it is recommended to be on high awareness and guard when one enters a room of any type. Aside from rooms, '''Level 575''' also possesses hallways which connect these rooms together. These hallways, similar to the rooms, vary in style, and styles present within certain hallways often match up with styles present in the closest rooms to those hallways. The halls, unlike the rooms, are not affected by the phenomenon which gives them high fragility; rather, they seem to contain overtly more strength than even a standard hallway within [[The Frontrooms]] would contain. Rarely, one may come across two hallways [[Noclipping|noclipped]] into each other. It is advised to avoid these instances, as they may be highly unstable. Lastly, '''Level 575''' has an outside area. It can be accessed by breaking a window within one of the rooms and jumping out, though this is highly advised against as one can never know how far up in the building one is. The second, safer, though harder way to get to the outside is by wandering long enough until one finds an elevator, which possesses only a down and up button; selecting the down button will take one to a hallway with an unlocked double door on the other end leading to the outside. The up button has been found to be ineffective if one is not on the ground floor; however, if one is on the ground floor, the up button will lead one back to the original area from which they entered the elevator. == The Outside == The outside of '''Level 575''' is nothing more than a seemingly endless grass field stretching in all directions; the endlessness is further confirmed by the fact that the building, while also seemingly endless in height, is finite in its length and width; one can travel around to the back of the building, which still depicts the same sight: an endless grass field. While outside, one can often spot flower pots hanging outside the windows of the building. These pots are, however, usually empty, as if the flowers in them were plucked out. Beside from such decor, the building of '''Level 575''' has been found to be quite large in its non-height dimensions, despite its finite size; this is because of its hallways, which are typically found in really long sizes, often stretching for miles on endThe record length for a recorded hallway is approximately 10 miles.. Other than overall being a strange experience to take in, the Outside does not really possess more interesting visuals. Furthermore, the area has been found to be slightly more dangerous than the building; this is because the Outside, and thus the entirety of '''Level 575''', is affected by a day/night cycle of about 10 daytime hours and seven nighttime hoursThis is keeping in mind that the times may be slightly inaccurate, due to the difference in time between [[The Frontrooms]] and the Backrooms.. While the outside may be safe during the day, hostile entities present within the building can often be seen leaving the building into the outside in large quantities once nightfall arrives. As a result, while both areas are still dangerous throughout one's time within '''Level 575''', the building has been found to be much more safer during nighttime, and the outside during daytime. During the entirety of time's passing within the outside of '''Level 575''', the light source responsible for lighting up the area during both day and nightThough, during night, the light outside is increasingly less visible. does not seem to be present. It is as if the light is invisible and yet still possesses all the similar effects a standard light would present. == Addendum == Aside from the sensitivity of its rooms, '''Level 575''' possesses another, more general phenomenon. As one's stay within the level prolongs, their anxiety and paranoia levels begin to rise. It is unknown why this happens, but the best theory is that '''Level 575''' absorbs way more psychic energy released from [[The Void]] than other levels doNote that high amounts of this energy can cause these exact effects of anxiety to rise.. Regardless of why one's anxiety and paranoia begins to rise, it is the core reason not to remain in '''Level 575''' for too long. The maximum amount of time spent here is recommended to be two hours. One could go further, but it seems that following two hours, the effects get worse; wanderers who stayed for over two hours reported experiencing events such as sleep paralysis for days after their exit from the level. Sleep paralysis and unhealthy amounts of stress are not the biggest consequences of a prolonged stay in '''Level 575'''. It has been found that if one spends about five hours or more in the level, even if they exit right after, they may slowly begin to go insane; it has been found that stays of such lengths often speed up the [[Phenomenon 35|Wretched Cycle]] to rapid progression that may be difficult to cure in time. Not to mention the possibility of hallucinations the longer one remains here. While the consequences of prolonged stay are one of the biggest dangers of '''Level 575''', there are numerous other strange properties. One less noticeable phenomenon is the strange way that lamps often disappear if a wanderer attempts to turn one on. The reasons for this are unknown, and it is not always guaranteed; furthermore, this phenomenon is often ignored, due to the absence of dangerous properties. A more noticeable and dangerous anomaly in the environment of '''Level 575''' is the ability of the level to shift itself around; this typically results in a more dangerous environment for those who walk the level's halls. A popular example of such an instance is when a wanderer who was in the level for about an hour suddenly found themselves stepping on a bear trap which was not previously there, as they claim. The wanderer did survive, though with a broken leg. Lastly, '''Level 575''' possesses a phenomenon which affects its environment as a whole. The level has often been found to mimic other levels in certain areas and moments of time. An example of such an instance could be a wanderer turning a corner to find themselves in [[Level 0|a yellow hellscape]], only to return back to '''Level 575''' after wandering the new environment. == Settlements == As of current, no colonies or outposts have been found to exist in '''Level 575'''. The organization that discovered it, the [[M.E.G.|Major Explorer Group]], set up a project shortly after discovery to open an outpost in the level. The outpost, named ''Camp Room-haven'', was planned to be operated by the Department of Exploration as a way to provide wanderers who found themselves in the level with safety. There was no further reason for this outpost in truth; '''Level 575''' was far away from any levels which enemies had settlements, so military involvement seemed unnecessary. The level had no incredibly strange anomalies that required large scientific research management. In short, the only purpose for ''Base Room-haven'' was to document this level into [[The Database]] and to allow wanderers safety within '''Level 575'''. The project was an eventual success, and the outpost remained in operation for almost a year, until the [[M.E.G.]] decided to evacuate it entirely and shut it down. This is completely for reasons undisclosed to the public. The [[M.E.G.]] has since not released any purpose for its actions, and as a result is often condemned by the public, which previously had favored ''Base Room-haven''. == Inhabitants == '''Level 575''' is highly infested by entities. The level does not possess any unique entities of its own, though the entities that occupy it are quite dangerous and turn it into a much more hostile environment. These entities are listed below, from common to rare: *[[Entity 3]]; *[[Entity 9]];Every Faceling instance within '''Level 575''' has been found to be a hostile version. *[[Entity 5]]; *[[Entity 10]]; *[[Entity 2]]. == Entrances & Exits == === Entrances === '''Level 575''' possesses only a single possible entrance. *Rarely, one may come across an apartment building door in [[The Hub]]. It leads to '''Level 575'''. === Exits === As of current, '''Level 575''' possesses five documented exits. *Finding a room with a TV and turning it on as a low chance of the TV simply displaying static. [[Noclipping]] into this static will send one to [[Level 12]]. *Damage made to a room may cause cracks in the floor, which lead to [[The Void]]. *[[Noclipping]] into hallways that are stuck inside each other may send one into any random level. *Rarely, the environment within '''Level 575''' may take the shape of a tunnel, and the entire level may transition into [[The Hub]]. *Wandering enough far away from the building, within the outside, may lead one eventually to [[Level 10]]. {{Footnotes}}
